reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_49]]
Speaker 2: I earned my graduate degree at Golden Gate University.

[[BLOCK:srt_50]]
Speaker 2: Another example is Bernard Tyson (Bernard Tyson), who served as chairman of Kaiser Health (Kaiser Health), a major healthcare organization headquartered in the Bay Area.

[[BLOCK:srt_51]]
Speaker 2: Bernard was widely respected not only for his outstanding leadership as a CEO, but also for his deep commitment to community building and public service.

[[BLOCK:srt_52]]
Speaker 2: He truly embodied our philosophy: achieving business success while creating meaningful value for society.

[[BLOCK:srt_53]]
Speaker 1: I’m reminded of another example. Solomon Darwin is now a professor at the Berkeley Hoss School of business (伯克利哈斯商学院). He earned his MBA (MBA) at Golden Gate University.

[[BLOCK:srt_54]]
Speaker 1: Before that, he was working as a janitor.

[[BLOCK:srt_55]]
Speaker 1: When other universities failed to recognize his potential, one professor at Golden Gate University saw something remarkable in him. We admitted him and nurtured his talent. He went on to become a highly regarded professor at Berkeley and a leading advocate of the “Smart Village” movement.

[[BLOCK:srt_56]]
Speaker 1: That’s the spirit of our institution—discovering talent, recognizing potential, and investing in people’s growth. Even when others overlook them, we are willing to give them the opportunity to thrive.

[[BLOCK:srt_57]]
Speaker 3: Wonderful—that’s truly inspiring.

[[BLOCK:srt_58]]
Speaker 3: Golden Gate University was one of the earliest institutions in the United States to launch online education.

[[BLOCK:srt_59]]
Speaker 3: Could you tell us how Golden Gate University’s online programs first began, and what major milestones you’ve achieved so far?

[[BLOCK:srt_60]]
Speaker 1: Many people don’t realize that Golden Gate University was among the very first universities to offer online degree programs. We were experimenting with online education back when computer screens displayed nothing but text.
